channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The AI scaffolding layer is collapsing. LlamaIndex's CEO explains what survives.
item.description: The scaffolding layer that developers once needed to ship LLM applications — indexing layers, query engines, retrieval pipelines, carefully orchestrated agent loops — is collapsing. And according to Jerry Liu, co-founder and CEO of LlamaIndex, that's not a problem. It's the point.“As a result, there's less of a need for frameworks to actually help users compose these deterministic workflows in a light and shallow manner,” Jerry Liu, co-founder and CEO of LlamaIndex, explains in a new VentureBeat Beyond the Pilot podcast. Context is becoming the moatLiu’s LlamaIndex is one of the foremost retri...

item.title: Redditor builds a PC inside an old CRT monitor — it looks like an ancient iMac, but runs Cyberpunk 2077 at 100fps in 1440p
item.description: This retro PC build that uses a burnt-out old monitor as a case is like an iMac on steroids.

item.title: iPhone 17e vs iPhone 17 : quel iPhone « abordable » choisir ?
item.description: Une petite lettre, ça change tout. Ne vous méprenez pas : l'iPhone 17e est bien la version « lite » de l'iPhone 17 classique. Une version plus abordable, naturellement amputée de certaines fonctionnalités phares, qui mérite toutefois votre intérêt. On fait le point dans ce nouveau comparatif !

item.title: LG's 2026 Gram laptop lineup starts at $1,150
item.description: They're available today from LG's website.

item.title: Amazon’s built-in AI price history expands to show the entire last year
item.description: Amazon's built-in price tracking feature now allows you to see how much a product's price has changed over the past year. To use the feature, open the Amazon app and select the "Price history" button next to the item's price, or ask Amazon's AI assistant Rufus. The expansion comes just weeks ahead of Amazon's annual […]

item.title: Dreame — the vacuum company — just ‘launched’ its own phones
item.description: Dreame, a Chinese manufacturer best known for its robot vacuums but with ambitions to do much more, says it's making smartphones now. I'm not sure I believe it. The company showed off two phones at its own Next event, which took place in California this week, though both had previously been revealed in China in […]

item.title: xAI launches Grok 4.3 at an aggressively low price and a new, fast, powerful voice cloning suite
item.description: While Elon Musk faces off against his former colleague and OpenAI co-founder Sam Altman in court, Musk's rival firm xAI, founded to take on OpenAI, isn't slowing down on launching competitive new products and services.Last night, xAI shipped a new, proprietary base large language model (LLM), Grok 4.3, and a new voice cloning suite on the web. The new products arrive after months of tumult from xAI that saw all of Musk's 10 original co-founders of the lab and dozens more researchers exit the firm and Grok was eclipsed on performance by many new competing LLMs from the likes of OpenAI, Anthropi...

item.title: It's Bandcamp Friday again
item.description: What are you buying today?

item.title: À moins de 90 € pendant les French Days, ces récents écouteurs Huawei avec ANC et audio spatial valent encore plus le détour
item.description: Les Huawei FreeBuds 6, successeurs des FreeBuds 5, sont des écouteurs sans fil proposant des fonctionnalités haut de gamme, à l'image de l'ANC et de l'audio spatial avec suivi de tête. Une paire de true wireless que l'on trouve d'ailleurs, pendant les French Days, à 89,99 euros au lieu de 159,99 euros sur Amazon.

item.title: 'Exactly one photon': European scientists have built the first block of a truly unhackable global quantum internet that can run on existing fiber networks — breaking what was once thought to be an impossible barrier to transmit identical light particles
item.description: Scientists break the telecom wavelength barrier that previously blocked long distance quantum communication over existing optical fiber infrastructure.

item.title: Rocket League has a new Easy Anti-Cheat addition, and it still works on the Steam Deck — it's about time for other game Devs to follow suit
item.description: SteamOS is on the verge of huge growth to its player base with the Steam Machine's arrival, and that's why this one factor can't be ignored by game developers.

item.title: This accessory can snap a Steam Controller to your phone — or almost anything else
item.description: Valve's new Steam Controller goes on sale on Monday for $99, and accessories-maker Mechanism will be ready. As far as we know, Mechanism's new Basegrip is the very first way to attach a Steam Controller to your phone - as well as Mechanism's lineup of accessories, including mounts for hanging handhelds and gamepads on the […]

item.title: 3 thrilling new Netflix shows you shouldn't miss in May 2026
item.description: Get your thrills with Netflix this May, as three great new originals are streaming throughout the month.

item.title: Players from the NBA, NFL, and MLB call for a ban on betting ‘unders’
item.description: The unions backing professional NBA, NFL, MLB, NHL, and MLS players are calling on the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to ban prediction market platforms from allowing users to bet on a player's underperformance or injury, Sports Business Journal reports. In their letter, the unions cite the need for "appropriate regulations" to protect athletes and […]...

item.title: Severe Linux Copy Fail security flaw uncovered using AI scanning help
item.description: Nearly every Linux distribution released since 2017 is currently vulnerable to a security bug called "Copy Fail" that allows any user to give themselves administrator privileges. The exploit, publicly disclosed as CVE-2026-31431 on Wednesday, uses a Python script that works across all of the vulnerable Linux distributions, requiring "no per-distro offsets, no version checks, no […]...

item.title: The Chinese Government Just Got the World’s Largest Digital Rights Conference Canceled
item.description: Access Now, the group that organizes RightsCon, says Zambian officials asked it to exclude Taiwanese participants if it wanted the event to proceed as planned.

item.title: Oura adds more detailed hormonal health insights to its Series 3 and 4 rings
item.description: The system will track potential side effects of various birth control methods.

item.title: Russia orders Apple and Google to remove Important Stories, an investigative media app that works without a VPN — leaving Russians without a key source for accessing uncensored news
item.description: Important Stories launched the IStories mobile app in February to make sure information would reach Russians despite widespread internet censorship and VPN restrictions.

item.title: Dreame’s rocket-powered car can do 0–60 in 0.9 seconds because you can just say things now
item.description: When I first heard that a vacuum company had released a rocket-powered electric vehicle with physically impossible-sounding performance specs, I immediately thought James Dyson was up to his old tricks again. Fortunately, I was wrong. This time the household appliance company trying its hand at super car design is Dreame (pronounced like "dreamy"), a little-known […]...

item.title: Asus’s Intel Core i7-powered ROG Strix G16 is a high-performance laptop for creators and gamers — and it’s currently $100 off
item.description: Asus’s ROG Strix G16 laptop pairs an Intel Core i7-14650HX processor with 16GB of DDR5 RAM and a 1TB SSD for creative tasks, work and entertainment.

item.title: Raising Cane’s is opening new locations this month and your city might be on the list
item.description: The chicken chain is expanding fast across seven states with new openings starting May 12. Here’s exactly where it’s landing next. Raising Cane’s, the Louisiana-based chicken finger chain known for its tangy sauce, crinkle fries, and thick Texas toast, is continuing to expand. This May, Cane’s will open locations in seven states, including its first in one....

item.title: We just got a new reason to believe the Trump phone is real(ish)
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. This time we've discovered a little more evidence that the phone is on its way to a release. Trump Mobile's T1 Phone has just passed another milestone on its slow […]

item.title: I saw Samsung's new mid-range OLED TV and its controversial Glare Free screen — and based on my first look, it could be a real challenger to the LG C6
item.description: The Samsung S90H is the LG C6's rival and while the latter has set a high bar, my first impressions with the S90H suggest it's more than able to reach that bar.

item.title: 'Your duty is not yet done' — Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2 is so popular that it's getting another year of content
item.description: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2 is getting a surprise third year of content.

item.title: This is not a drill: Terry Crews has come to Call of Duty
item.description: Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 Season 3 Reloaded has arrived and with it comes Terry Crews.

item.title: 'The cost of cloud services and the cost of AI will also go down in the future, so we’ll try very hard to keep it for free': Coros' CEO on how the smartwatch maker is swerving subscriptions for now — avoiding another Garmin Connect+ disaster
item.description: How Coros has "no plans" to implement unpopular Garmin- or Whoop-style subscription-only features anytime soon, according to its CEO

item.title: QR code phishing surges 146% as Microsoft detects and analyzes 8.3 billion phishing threats in Q1 2026 – attackers are changing tactics to bypass security
item.description: Microsoft has seen attackers changing tactics to get around security tools, and they keep adapting even when disrupted.

item.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
item.description: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.
